visualizerParams.title: Anagram Space Handling Bug
visualizerParams.problemDescription: The anagram checker is failing for strings that contain different numbers of spaces, even if the actual letters match. Identify and fix the flaw in how characters are processed.

solution.code:
from collections import Counter

def are_anagrams(s1, s2):
    c1 = Counter(s1.lower().replace(' ', ''))
    c2 = Counter(s2.lower().replace(' ', ''))
    return c1 == c2

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(f"'a b', 'ab': {are_anagrams('a b', 'ab')}")
solution.explanation: Anagrams ignore spaces. The buggy code included spaces in the frequency count, so 'a b' (with one space) was not considered an anagram of 'ab' (with zero spaces). Removing spaces before comparison fixes the issue.
